---
title: "Simulation of IHPP for Wiebull intensity function"
author: "4 MA"
date: "2025/2026"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r simulPPh1,eval=FALSE}
simulPPh1 <- function(lambda,Tmax)
{
  N <- rpois(1,lambda*Tmax)
  U <- runif(N,0,Tmax)
  
    return(sort(U))
}
```

```{r plot.PP}
plot.PP<- function(PP)
{
  # plot the counting process (with jumps of size 1 (starting at point (0,0))):
  plot(c(0,PP),0:length(PP),type="s",xlab="time t",ylab="number of events by time t")
  # add the arrival times on the horizontal axis: 
  points(PP,0*PP,type="p",pch=16)
  # link arrival times with the counts:
  lines(PP,0:(length(PP)-1),type="h",lty=2)
}
```

# 1 - Inhomogeneous Poisson processes

Simulation of an inhomogeneous Poisson processes with given intensity
function `lambda_fct` on a fixed window [0,`Tmax`].

```{r simulPPi,eval=FALSE}
simulPPi = function(lambda_fct,Tmax,M)
{
  M= lambda_fct(Tmax)
  res = NULL
  PPh <- simulPPh1(M, Tmax)
  U <- runif(length(PPh),0,M)
  for (i in 1:length(PPh)){
    if (U[i]< lambda_fct(PPh[i]))
      {
      res <- append(res, PPh[i])
    }
  }
    return(sort(res))
  }
```

### NHPP Simulation

```{r simu, eval=FALSE, warning=TRUE}

Tmax=100

 
lambda_fct1 <- function(x){
  beta = 1.09
  teta = 1
  return((beta/teta)*(x/teta)^(beta-1))}



PPi1 = simulPPi(lambda_fct1, Tmax)
```

## Crow

```{r lambda1,eval=FALSE}


# plot:
par(mfrow=c(1,2))
plot.PP(PPi1)
curve(lambda_fct1,from=0,to=Tmax,n=1000)

```

### Test Statistic :

```{r teststat,eval=FALSE}

testCrow <- function(PP,Tmax)
{
  stat_test_Obs = 2*sum(log(Tmax/PP)) 
  
  p_value <-pchisq((stat_test_Obs),2*length(PP))
  
  print(paste("The Observed test Statistic is : ",stat_test_Obs))
  print(paste("The p-value : ",p_value))

  
  if (p_value< 0.05){
    print("we reject H0 at level 5%, B is  greater than 1 ")}
    else {
        print("we do not reject H0 at level 5%, the data can't denied the fact that B could be 1 ")}
    
    
  

  return(p_value)
}




testCrow(PPi1, Tmax)

```

## Barlow

### Test Statistic :

```{r}

testBarlow <- function(PP,Tmax)
{
  n =length(PP)
  d= floor(n/2)
  
  stat_test_Obs = ((n-d)*PP[d])/(d*(PP[n]-PP[d]))
  
  p_value <- 1- pf((stat_test_Obs),2*d, 2*(n-d))
  
  print(paste("The Observed test Statistic is : ",stat_test_Obs))
  print(paste("The p-value : ",p_value))

  
  if (p_value< 0.05){
    print("we reject H0 at level 5%, B is  greater than 1 ")}
    else {
        print("we do not reject H0 at level 5%, the data can't denied the fact that B could be 1 ")}
    
    
  

  return(p_value)
}

testBarlow(PPi1)






```

# Power Comparaison

```{r}
plot.level.power1 <- function(Tmax,beta0,TrueB,alpha,nsimu)
{
  plot(range(TrueLambda),c(alpha,alpha),ylim=c(0,1),xlab="True Beta",ylab="Level/Power",
       type="l",col="red",main=paste("Beta0= ",beta0,",  Tmax = ",Tmax,sep=""))
  abline(1,0,lty=2,col="blue")
  
  for(lambda in TrueLambda)
  {
    # estimating the proportion under lambda in TrueLambda
    propReject=0
    for(sim in 1:nsimu){
      propReject=propReject+(testCrow(simulPPh1(b,Tmax),Tmax,b0) <= alpha)/nsimu
    }
  }
}
alpha=0.05
nsimu=1000
b0=1.5
TrueB=c(1,1.5,1.8,1.9,2.2,2.5,3)

par(mfrow=c(1,2))
for(Tmax in c(1,10,100,500))
{
  plot.level.power1(Tmax,b0, TrueB, alpha, nsimu)
}
```
